turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#0a2430"/>
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="goldBar" x1="0" x2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#ffd34d"/>
      <stop offset="0.5" stop-color="#ffb84d"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#b78600"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="coinGrad" cx="35%" cy="30%" r="70%">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#fff8e0"/>
      <stop offset="0.25" stop-color="#ffed7f"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#c58b00"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="6" result="b"/>
      <feMerge><feMergeNode in="b"/><feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/></feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- subtle ground shadow -->
  <ellipse cx="330" cy="360" rx="110" ry="28" fill="#000" opacity="0.35"/>

  <!-- gold bar, slightly tilted -->
  <g transform="rotate(-12 200 180)">
    <rect x="120" y="120" width="260" height="120" rx="20" ry="20" fill="url(#goldBar)" stroke="#8b5f00" stroke-width="4"/>
    <rect x="125" y="125" width="250" height="20" rx="10" ry="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.35)"/>
    <ellipse cx="320" cy="200" rx="50" ry="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.15)" />
  </g>

  <!-- stack of gold coins -->
  <g transform="translate(320,300)">
    <g transform="translate(0,40)">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="120" rx="62" ry="16" fill="#000" opacity="0.25"/>
    </g>
    <g>
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="48" fill="url(#coinGrad)" stroke="#a06e00" stroke-width="4"/>
      <ellipse cx="-14" cy="-16" rx="22" ry="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"/>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(10,60)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="40" fill="url(#coinGrad)" stroke="#a06e00" stroke-width="4"/>
      <ellipse cx="-10" cy="-12" rx="18" ry="8" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.5)"/>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(-18,48) rotate(-8)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="36" fill="url(#coinGrad)" stroke="#a06e00" stroke-width="3"/>
      <ellipse cx="-8" cy="-10" rx="16" ry="7" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.45)"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- sparkles to suggest shine -->
  <g fill="#fff8b3" opacity="0.95">
    <polygon points="88,60 96,72 110,76 96,80 88,92 80,80 66,76 80,72" transform="translate(360,120) scale(0.9)"/>
    <polygon points="88,60 96,72 110,76 96,80 88,92 80,80 66,76 80,72" transform="translate(420,180) scale(0.6)"/>
    <polygon points="88,60 96,72 110,76 96,80 88,92 80,80 66,76 80,72" transform="translate(220,140) scale(0.7) rotate(30 88 76)"/>
    <circle cx="240" cy="120" r="6" fill="#fff" opacity="0.9"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#0a2430"/>
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="goldBar" x1="0" x2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#ffd34d"/>
      <stop offset="0.5" stop-color="#ffb84d"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#b78600"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="coinGrad" cx="35%" cy="30%" r="70%">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#fff8e0"/>
      <stop offset="0.25" stop-color="#ffed7f"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#c58b00"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="6" result="b"/>
      <feMerge><feMergeNode in="b"/><feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/></feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- subtle ground shadow -->
  <ellipse cx="330" cy="360" rx="110" ry="28" fill="#000" opacity="0.35"/>

  <!-- gold bar, slightly tilted -->
  <g transform="rotate(-12 200 180)">
    <rect x="120" y="120" width="260" height="120" rx="20" ry="20" fill="url(#goldBar)" stroke="#8b5f00" stroke-width="4"/>
    <rect x="125" y="125" width="250" height="20" rx="10" ry="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.35)"/>
    <ellipse cx="320" cy="200" rx="50" ry="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.15)" />
  </g>

  <!-- stack of gold coins -->
  <g transform="translate(320,300)">
    <g transform="translate(0,40)">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="120" rx="62" ry="16" fill="#000" opacity="0.25"/>
    </g>
    <g>
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="48" fill="url(#coinGrad)" stroke="#a06e00" stroke-width="4"/>
      <ellipse cx="-14" cy="-16" rx="22" ry="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"/>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(10,60)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="40" fill="url(#coinGrad)" stroke="#a06e00" stroke-width="4"/>
      <ellipse cx="-10" cy="-12" rx="18" ry="8" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.5)"/>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(-18,48) rotate(-8)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="36" fill="url(#coinGrad)" stroke="#a06e00" stroke-width="3"/>
      <ellipse cx="-8" cy="-10" rx="16" ry="7" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.45)"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- sparkles to suggest shine -->
  <g fill="#fff8b3" opacity="0.95">
    <polygon points="88,60 96,72 110,76 96,80 88,92 80,80 66,76 80,72" transform="translate(360,120) scale(0.9)"/>
    <polygon points="88,60 96,72 110,76 96,80 88,92 80,80 66,76 80,72" transform="translate(420,180) scale(0.6)"/>
    <polygon points="88,60 96,72 110,76 96,80 88,92 80,80 66,76 80,72" transform="translate(220,140) scale(0.7) rotate(30 88 76)"/>
    <circle cx="240" cy="120" r="6" fill="#fff" opacity="0.9"/>
  </g>
</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Creating valid JSON**

I need to manually produce JSON with an SVG string value. I should escape newline characters for safety, even though many JSON serializers allow raw newlines. But just to be clear, a valid JSON string can’t contain literal newlines; they must be escaped as 
. So, I’ll replace any newlines with 
 and escape quotes with ". Alternatively, I could create a single-line string with embedded 
 sequences. It’s essential to ensure that there are no invalid characters. Let's get started!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
